title: Lobbying and Influence
shortSummary: Learn how corporations, unions, NGOs, peak bodies, and advocates try to move government through meetings, submissions, media, coalitions, and timing.
whyItMatters: Politics is not only formal institutions. Influence enters through relationships, submissions, campaigns, and pressure. If you do not understand this layer, you misunderstand how policy actually moves.
coreConcepts: Industry groups, unions, NGOs, and peak bodies
Ministerial offices, departments, and access points
The line between influence and corruption
mentalModels: Influence is normal; corruption is the boundary problem
Timing and access often matter as much as argument quality
Most influence happens before the public fight is visible

goDeeper.label: See the lobbying campaign project
goDeeper.description: Map actors, pressure points, and integrity risks around a real issue.

goDeeper.label: Open the corporate affairs day-in-the-life
goDeeper.description: See how influence work actually feels inside a working week.
